authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Deer Creek Reach A — Upper Deer Creek Falls to Deer Creek Canyon mouth
reaches.fromDesc: 250 feet below Upper Deer Creek Falls and fishway (1.5 miles upstream from Potato Patch Campground)
reaches.toDesc: U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Deer Creek Canyon

reaches.name: Deer Creek Reach B — Deer Creek Canyon mouth to mouth of Deer Creek
reaches.fromDesc: U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Deer Creek Canyon
reaches.toDesc: Mouth of Deer Creek (confluence with the Sacramento River near Vina)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(35) — Deer Creek (Tehama Co.)
sources.quotedText: (35) Deer Creek (Tehama Co.). (A) From 250 feet below Upper Deer Creek Falls and fishway (located 1.5 miles upstream from Potato Patch Campground) downstream 31 miles to U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Deer Creek Canyon (see Section 2.35 for closure at Upper Deer Creek Falls). Last Sat. in Apr through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) From U.S. Geological Survey gauging station cable crossing at mouth of Deer Creek Canyon downstream to mouth of Deer Creek. Jun. 16 through Sep. 30. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — footnote definition of hatchery trout/steelhead (**)
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
Deer Creek Study (Tehama County) — CDFW Instream Flow Program
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 1655075, "Deer Creek", Stream, CA, Tehama Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":1655075,"gaz_name":"Deer Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Tehama","objectid_1":1390169}. Corrects prior file coordinates (-121.98, 40.02), which appear to have been a rounded/guessed estimate from the reach description rather than a GNIS pull — the corrected point (-122.08167, 39.92639) is the creek's mouth at its confluence with the Sacramento River near Vina, matching the GNIS multipoint stream geometry's mouth-end vertex and corroborated by the Wikipedia infobox coordinate for Deer Creek (Tehama County, California) (39°55′35″N 122°04′54″W / 39.92639°N 122.08167°W).

regulations.humanSummary: Reach A (below Upper Deer Creek Falls to Deer Creek Canyon mouth) open last Saturday in April through November 15.
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr through Nov. 15.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used in Reach A.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, Reach A.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Reach B (Deer Creek Canyon mouth to mouth of Deer Creek) open June 16 through September 30.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, Reach B.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only hatchery trout or steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained; wild (unclipped) trout or steelhead must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released immediately; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish count toward the bag.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

parameters.note: Section 2.35 provides for a closure at Upper Deer Creek Falls and fishway; only the reach 250 feet below the falls downstream is open under this entry.

regulations.humanSummary: Upper Deer Creek Falls and fishway area is closed per Section 2.35; open water begins 250 feet below the falls.
regulations.verbatimText: (see Section 2.35 for closure at Upper Deer Creek Falls).

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
